findings.description: The NC pine tree organizational logo/emblem ('Energy, Mineral & Land Resources / ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY') prominently displayed in the upper left of PDF page 1 is missing from the HTML rendering.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a green NC-shaped logo with a white pine tree silhouette and agency text beneath it. The HTML rendering shows the colored banner bar and official names text but no logo image.

findings.description: Repeated header/footer content inserted between page-equivalent sections disrupts reading flow. Between the memorandum cover content and the History/Background section, the HTML inserts the page footer ('State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality…'), a colored banner bar, and the official names block (PAT McCRORY, DONALD R. VAN DER VAART, TRACY DAVIS).
findings.evidence: In the continuous HTML flow, after the memo outline (I–IV) and before 'I. History/Background', a reader encounters the full page footer text, a colored bar, and the repeated government officials' header block. In the PDF, these are simply page chrome on separate pages and do not interrupt reading.

findings.description: A decorative page-border image from the PDF page footers is inserted mid-content between the memo header section and Section I (History/Background), breaking body reading flow.
findings.evidence:
HTML has placed between (memo header) and the
containing 'I. History/Background'. This colored bar appears at the bottom of every PDF page and should not appear in the body content flow.
